title: Reported Speech
subtitle: Backshifting, reporting verbs, and the grammar of attribution
intro: Reported speech is how you attribute ideas without quoting them. Direct quotation is rare in academic writing; paraphrased attribution is the norm. The classic mistake is to copy the speaker's tense: 'The minister said he will resign' instead of 'The minister said he would resign.' The rule is to backshift when the reporting verb is past.

branches.label: Reporting verbs
children: Choose verb that colours the stance

rules.heading: Backshift by one step when reporting in the past
rules.body: Present → past; past → past perfect; will → would; can → could; must → had to. 'I am leaving' → 'She said she was leaving.'

rules.heading: No backshift for timeless truths
rules.body: 'The earth orbits the sun' → 'She said (that) the earth orbits the sun' — still present.

rules.heading: Reporting verbs carry meaning
rules.body: 'Say' is neutral; 'claim' signals doubt; 'argue' signals position; 'admit' signals concession; 'announce' signals formal public statement; 'suggest' signals proposal.

rules.heading: Questions and commands
rules.body: Questions: if/whether + statement order: 'He asked whether I had finished.' Commands: tell + object + to + V: 'The teacher told us to rewrite the essay.'

examples.sentence: Researchers have argued that social mobility in many developed countries has stagnated since the 1980s.
The report warned that failure to invest in infrastructure would have long-term consequences.
Economists maintain that moderate inflation is healthy for a growing economy.
My professor once told me that learning a language requires forgetting your fear of mistakes.
examples.note: Past reporting with no backshift on the general truth.

examples.sentence: The committee recommended that the bill be amended before its second reading.
examples.note: 'Recommend that' + bare infinitive — subjunctive form popular in formal register.

band6.text: The researcher said that 'technology is the future'.
band6.issue: Uses quotation marks + 'said' — feels like direct-speech copy-paste, no reporting frame.

band8.text: The researcher argued that technology would play a decisive role in the coming decades.
band8.why: 'argued' colours stance, 'would play' backshifts future accurately, and 'decisive role' replaces flat 'is the future'.

band8_marker: Band 8 writers vary reporting verbs to encode attitude and authority: 'contend', 'maintain', 'posit', 'caution', 'concede', 'assert', 'demonstrate'. They also use noun-phrase attribution ('The rationale behind the policy is…', 'According to the latest OECD report…') to avoid repetitive 'says' / 'said' structures.

common_errors.fix: 'Say' never takes an indirect object without 'to'; 'tell' requires an object: 'He said to me / He told me that…'

common_errors.error: Forgetting to backshift: 'She said she is tired.'
Question-form word order: 'He asked me where was I.'
common_errors.fix: → 'He asked me where I was.' (Statement order after a reporting verb.)

practice.title: Direct to reported
practice.instruction: Rewrite as reported speech, backshifting tenses where needed.

items.prompt: 'I have never smoked,' he claimed. — He claimed __________.
'We will review the decision,' the board announced. — The board announced __________.
items.answer: that they would review the decision

items.prompt: 'Are you attending the conference?' she asked me. — She asked me __________.
items.answer: whether I was attending the conference

items.prompt: 'Submit your essays by Friday,' the tutor said. — The tutor told us __________.
items.answer: to submit our essays by Friday

items.prompt: 'I cannot confirm that yet,' the spokesperson admitted. — The spokesperson admitted __________.
items.answer: that she / he could not confirm that yet

items.prompt: 'Plastic waste is polluting the oceans,' the scientist warned. — The scientist warned __________.
items.answer: that plastic waste was polluting the oceans

items.prompt: 'Why did the policy fail?' the journalist asked. — The journalist asked __________.
items.explanation: Past Simple → Past Perfect; question → statement order.

items.prompt: 'Water freezes at zero degrees,' the teacher explained. — The teacher explained __________.
items.answer: that water freezes at zero degrees

practice.title: Pick the reporting verb and form
practice.instruction: Type the verb in brackets in the correct reported-speech form.

items.prompt: The minister (deny) ___ any wrongdoing when questioned by reporters.
Witnesses (claim) ___ that they had seen the suspect near the scene.
items.explanation: Past reporting with Past Perfect in the reported clause.

items.prompt: She (insist) ___ that she had paid the bill in full.
The director (announce) ___ that a new branch (open) ___ next year.
Experts (caution) ___ that the proposal (risk) ___ unintended consequences.
items.explanation: Backshift acceptable; timeless risk analysis can stay present.

items.prompt: He (suggest) ___ that we (postpone) ___ the meeting.
The researchers (demonstrate) ___ that the drug (reduce) ___ symptoms by 40%.
items.explanation: Backshift optional for established scientific finding.

items.prompt: She (ask) ___ me how long I (wait) ___ .
Direct: 'I am going to resign.' Reported:
options: He said he is going to resign.
He said he was going to resign.
He said that he resign.
He told that he was going to resign.

items.prompt: Direct: 'Can you help me?' Reported:
options: She asked me can I help her.
She asked me that I could help her.
She asked if I could help her.
She asked to me if I can help.

items.prompt: Direct: 'The earth is round.' Reported (teacher's lesson):
options: The teacher said the earth was round.
The teacher said that the earth is round.
The teacher told the earth is round.
The teacher explained the earth being round.
items.explanation: Timeless truth → no backshift (either is accepted, present is preferred for truths).

items.prompt: Direct: 'Don’t touch that,' the guard said. Reported:
options: The guard told not to touch that.
The guard said me don’t touch that.
The guard told me not to touch that.
The guard said that I don’t touch that.

items.prompt: Which verb best conveys reluctant acknowledgement?
options: claimed
admitted
announced
argued
items.explanation: 'Admit' signals concession of a fact the speaker would prefer to deny.

items.prompt: Which verb best introduces a formal public statement?
options: whispered
announced
muttered
admitted

items.prompt: Direct: 'We have been working on this for years.' Reported:
options: They said they are working on it for years.
They said they had been working on it for years.
They said they worked on it for years.
They told they have been working on it.
items.explanation: Present Perfect Continuous → Past Perfect Continuous.

items.prompt: Which is correct?
options: He said me that he would come.
He told that he would come.
He said that he would come.
He told to me he would come.
items.explanation: 'Say' does not take an indirect object without 'to'.
